skill.name: specdd-debug
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to diagnose or fix a bug against active SpecDD specs.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through a structured workflow for debugging against active SpecDD specifications and contracts.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: All 15 static analysis findings are false positives. The 13 external_commands detections were triggered by markdown backtick code spans (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`) misidentified as Ruby shell backtick execution. The 2 weak cryptographic algorithm detections matched plain text unrelated to any cryptographic operation. The SKILL.md file is a pure markdown instruction document with no executable code, no network calls, no file system access, and no command execution. The skill is safe to publish.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Formatting Misidentified as Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. Static analyzer detected Ruby shell backtick execution at 13 locations in SKILL.md. All instances are markdown inline code spans (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`, `.specdd/bootstrap.project.md`, `Must`, `Scenario`, `Handles`, `Returns`, `Raises`) used for documentation formatting. The file contains no shell execution commands. This is a well-known false positive pattern when scanning markdown files with regex-based static analyzers.

low_findings.title: Plain Text Misidentified as Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. Static analyzer flagged lines 3 and 58 for weak cryptographic algorithm usage. Line 3 is YAML frontmatter `description` field text. Line 58 is a documentation sentence about asking before editing specs. Neither line contains any cryptographic operation, hash function, algorithm name, or cipher reference. This is a likely pattern-matching false positive.

content.value_statement: Debugging SpecDD projects requires precise workflow adherence to bootstrap and spec chain rules. This skill guides AI agents through bootstrap resolution, spec chain analysis, and root cause identification so debugging follows project contracts correctly.
actual_capabilities: Resolve bootstrap chain in correct order for SpecDD projects
Walk ancestor spec chain from content root to target
Compare observed behavior against Must, Scenario, and Handles entries
Identify implementation drift, test drift, spec ambiguity, and missing coverage
Fix root causes within write authority boundaries
Report contract used, root cause, files changed, and verification result
limitations: Requires an existing SpecDD project with a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file
Cannot bootstrap or adopt SpecDD in a non-SpecDD project
Cannot change specifications to match broken behavior without user request
Does not infer authority from filenames or conventions unless explicitly mapped in bootstrap

use_cases.title: Debug Spec Failure for Developers
use_cases.description: A developer encounters a test failure that may indicate implementation drift from the governing SpecDD specification. The skill guides the AI to compare observed behavior against spec entries and identify the root cause.

use_cases.title: Resolve Spec Ambiguity for QA Engineers
use_cases.description: A QA engineer finds that test behavior does not match expected spec outcomes. The skill helps determine whether the issue is spec ambiguity or missing spec coverage.

use_cases.title: Audit Bug Fix for Tech Leads
use_cases.description: A tech lead needs to verify that a bug fix stays within write authority boundaries and does not silently weaken project contracts. The skill enforces correct bootstrap and spec chain resolution.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Spec Debug
prompt_templates.prompt: Debug the failure in this SpecDD project. First resolve the bootstrap chain, then compare observed behavior against the governing spec entries.
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial debugging request when a spec-related failure is detected

prompt_templates.title: Root Cause Classification
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the current failure and classify it as implementation drift, test drift, spec ambiguity, or missing spec coverage. Explain your reasoning for each classification.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep analysis to categorize the type of spec deviation

prompt_templates.title: Write Authority Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Identify the nearest local spec that grants write authority before making any edits. List the authority boundaries and confirm which files can be modified.
prompt_templates.scenario: Before implementing a fix to verify modification permissions

prompt_templates.title: Full Debug Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Reproduce the failure, compare against applicable Must and Scenario entries, fix the smallest root cause inside write authority, and add focused regression verification. Report the contract used, root cause, files changed, and verification result.

output_examples.input: Debug the failing test in the payment processing module against its SpecDD spec.
output_examples.output: Bootstrap chain resolved: .specdd/bootstrap.md -> .specdd/bootstrap.project.md. Governing spec: payment/processing.spec.md Line 42: Must return 422 for invalid amount. Observed behavior: returns 500. Root cause: implementation drift in validation handler. Fix applied inside write authority. Verification: regression test covers the fix.

output_examples.input: Is the failing acceptance test caused by spec ambiguity or implementation drift?
output_examples.output: Root cause: Spec ambiguity. The governing spec Handles negative amounts but does not define the lower boundary. Two implementations are both spec-compliant. Recommendation: clarify the boundary with the product owner before fixing code.

best_practices: Always resolve the bootstrap chain in the correct order before analyzing spec behavior
Reproduce or inspect the failure before changing any code
Report the full contract chain, root cause, files changed, and verification result
anti_patterns: Inferring write authority from similar filenames or conventions not defined in the bootstrap
Changing specifications to match broken behavior without an explicit user request
Rereading the entire spec chain when only a narrow relevant spec section is needed

faq.question: What is a bootstrap chain and why does it matter?
faq.answer: The bootstrap chain defines the hierarchy of configuration files for a SpecDD project. Files are read in order from bootstrap.md to bootstrap.local.md. Later files may narrow but not weaken earlier constraints.

faq.question: Can this skill create new SpecDD specifications?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed only for debugging against existing specifications. Use the specdd-adopt skill to add SpecDD to a new or existing project.

faq.question: What if the spec and observed behavior conflict?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the AI to ask before editing the spec. Specifications should not be changed to match broken behavior unless the user explicitly requests a spec change.

faq.question: Does this skill execute any shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a pure instruction document with no executable code. It provides workflow guidance for AI agents only.

faq.question: What types of spec issues can this skill identify?
faq.answer: The skill can classify issues into four categories: implementation drift, test drift, spec ambiguity, and missing spec coverage.

faq.question: Does the skill work without an existing SpecDD project?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires an existing project with a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file. It will not proceed if this file is missing.
